message:
feat(fuzz): keyring (add_key/request_key/keyctl) harness (bd-ris3r)

crates/frankenlibc-fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_keyring.rs covers the Linux
kernel keyring family. add_key + request_key drive a small allowlist
of safe key types ("user", "logon", "keyring", "big_key", "asymmetric")
plus a fuzz-string slot for the unknown-type EINVAL branch. Payload
buffer is guarded with 32-byte sentinels on both sides, capped at 256
bytes. keyctl walks 33 KEYCTL_* commands plus an invalid sentinel.

Self-caught on first run: keyctl args interpreted as buffer sizes
(KEYCTL_READ length, KEYCTL_DH_COMPUTE out_len, KEYCTL_PKEY_*) tripped
the libfuzzer OOM ceiling when fuzzer fed gigabyte-scale args. Bound
all 4 args modulo 16KB before dispatch.

Verified via rch with CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_libc_cc_ris3r:
5.3M runs at 87K exec/s over 60s, no crashes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
